message:
mcp-server: clear lingering compiler warnings (4 fixes)

Iter 42's sanity sweep flagged 5 warnings that have been cluttering
every cargo test run since iter 22 era. None were bugs but they
masked any new warning that might surface in real refactoring.

Fixes:
  src/auth.rs            — `Capability` was only used in tests; moved
                          import behind #[cfg(test)] so non-test
                          builds don't see it as unused.
  src/http.rs            — `principal_for_client_cert` was imported
                          from mtls but never referenced in http.rs;
                          deleted from the use list.
  src/ratelimit.rs       — `Inner::last_reset` field is initialized
                          but the eviction trigger that reads it is
                          still scaffolded (v0.11). Annotated with
                          #[allow(dead_code)] + a one-line comment
                          pointing at the v0.11 plan.
  src/main.rs            — `let mut transport: Option<Transport> =
                          None;` had a dead initial assignment
                          (line 321 unconditionally reassigns before
                          line 348 reads). Changed to deferred decl
                          `let transport: Option<Transport>;` so the
                          single later assignment is the only one.
  tests/http_e2e.rs      — `let server = make_server();` at line 286
                          was immediately shadowed by another
                          `let server = ...::from_lake_with_caps(...)`.
                          Deleted the dead first call.

Verified: cargo test --release on mcp-server now emits ZERO warnings
across lib + bin + 4 integration test binaries (40 lib + 8 + 20 tests
all pass).

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>
